DEDICATION

Their views and tips are useful indeed. Unfortunately, it is not possible to list all of them in this limited space. I am grateful to all my family members.

ABSTRACT

This system-development paper specified for the sports technology field addresses a critical problem : casual players abandon their favorite sports due to lack of suitable sport’s partners and face challenges to find a matching team. The GameOn platform implements Online matching system using geolocation and skill-level algorithms to connect players and teams together, The development process follows a traditional plan-driven methodology (SDLC) with Next.js (web), and Node.js (backend). Key innovations include NLP-powered performance tips (DeepSeek API) and one-team ownership with unlimited joining features. This research strongly concludes that dynamic team formation systems significantly increase sports participation when combined with personalized training guidance.

ABSTRAK

Kertas pembangunan sistem ini yang ditetapkan untuk bidang teknologi sukan menangani masalah kritikal: pemain kasual meninggalkan sukan kegemaran mereka kerana kekurangan rakan sukan yang sesuai dan menghadapi cabaran untuk mencari pasukan yang sepadan. Platform GameOn mengimplementasikan sistem pemadanan dalam talian menggunakan geolokasi dan algoritma tahap kemahiran untuk menghubungkan pemain dan pasukan bersama, proses pembangunan mengikuti metodologi berasaskan pelan tradisional (SDLC) dengan Next.js (web), dan Node.js (backend). Inovasi utama termasuk petua prestasi berkuasa NLP (DeepSeek API) dan pemilikan satu pasukan dengan ciri penyertaan tanpa had. Penyelidikan ini dengan jelas menyimpulkan bahawa sistem pembentukan pasukan dinamik secara signifikan meningkatkan penyertaan dalam sukan apabila digabungkan dengan panduan latihan yang diperibadikan.

CHAPTER 1

INTRODUCTION

1.1  Introduction

GameOn Sports team Matcher is a game changer for casual players that cannot practice their favourite sports due to the challenges they face looking for a partner or a team that matches their needs. The GameOn sports connects players and allows for team formations, an efficient matching based on skill level and location, listing down all the available teams for the players. The platform is not limited to a certain sport (e.g. team sports, individual sports) or even an outdoor activity such as hiking.

1.2         Problem Background

While so many people share a great joy playing their favorite sports, Not all of them share the same competitive features(e.g. age, gender, skills ) or even the same locations, such complications are the joy cut-off for a lot of individuals.

Sports enthusiasts often struggle to find a suitable team,Even then the team might not be most suitable in terms of their skill level or preference. However, due to  limited options they often settle down for whatever is available. It's a real disappointment when you're trying to play your favorite sport, but finding the right people feels like a mission impossible. Such challenges will leave potential profits for sport venues to the contrast of having a systematic way for team formation.

1.3        Project Aim

GameOn connects players with other players based on their skill-level and location.

1.4         Project Objectives

  1. To elicit requirements specifications for the GameOn matching process through searching, filtering and joining Games, from the specified end users and owner.
  2. To design the conceptual and logical structure of the web based system (GameOn) interrupting the requirements elicited into the designs.
  3. To develop the core functionalities of the system specified through designs agreed on by the stakeholders.
  4. To test the basic functionality of the GameOn, Utilizing software quality assurance principles  through each system module.

1.5        Project Scope

The project includes the creation of a site that assists athletes in that they can form teams, get connected with the right players and enhance their sporting performance through AI. The project is meant to focus on the following areas :

Scope 1: User Profile and Team management

Enable user profile creation, where a user will be able to select the sports, he/she is interested in and write about their skills (beginner, intermediate, advanced).

Allow members to build sports teams, mentioning the required details, such as the team name, the sport, the team’s skills, and the place.

Help users connect with other player via teams that suit them in terms of skill level and location.

Provide ways to manage your game, by seamlessly customizing team information and accepting members after viewing them.

Scope 2:  APIs and External Services

implement Geolocation services for players to discover other teams or players near them, according to the player’s current location.

Personalized AI-powered guidance on training should be given based on what sport someone enjoys and how skilled they are.

Provide a seamless system for searching through and filtering player and teams.

Exclusions :

Direct booking from the app to a venue is not possible. users should book places for their events on their own.

Payment processing systems are excluded; Payments are out of current scope though it could be included as a future feature.

1.6          Project Importance

The proposed website helps solve big problems people who love sports deal with, and makes it easy for players to find each other, play, and get better together. By following the problem that needed solving, the project is able to give real benefits in these main ways:

Enhanced Accessibility and Connection:                                                        

The platform makes it easier for people to join in by letting them connect with other players or teams by sport preference and skill. This means that people, no matter how old they are or what gender they identify with, can more easily find or join teams that fit what they want, so they don't feel stuck with teams that might not be a good match.

Personalized Sports Experience:                                                 

Through user profiles and AI-powered training help, the website gives people the support they need to take part in sports at the level they like, and also gives them tips to get better at what they enjoy doing. This personalization makes users happier with what they are doing and also helps them stick with sports over time.

Community Building and Engagement:                                                 

By giving ways for people to make teams, talk with each other, and keep everything organized, the platform helps sports fans feel like a group and brings them together. It turns what can feel like a hard job into a fun and teamwork activity, so users stay encouraged and feel like they fit in.

Time and Effort Efficiency:                                                 

The geolocation-based search and easy-to-use interface make it easier and faster for users to find and join teams. Instead of picking something that doesn’t work well, users can easily find the right options, which helps more people take part in sports and have fun doing them. This project is important because it helps make sports more fun by fixing problems like teams that are too big or small, making sure people have easier access to sports, and giving players personal attention, all to make sports a better experience for everyone who loves them.

1.7         Report Organization

The report is organized with additional sections, including the title page, declaration, dedication, acknowledgment, abstract, table of contents, appendices, as well as lists of figures and tables. This framework enables effortless exploration and allows interested parties to promptly review the progress, results and conclusions relating to the project.

CHAPTER 2

LITERATURE REVIEW

2.1        Introduction

The increased popularity of sports as a tool to improve physical condition and build social networks has highlighted the existence of serious obstacles in the process of forming teams and matching players. Through a survey I have carried out among UTM students, at which 27 athletic students contributed, It reported that almost half of the participating teammates struggle to match with those of their own skill level, 37% lack beginner-friendly alternatives and 34% have issues with the remote game locations and lack of good communication tools. These results indicate the challenges that sports lovers face when trying to meet other compatible individuals. The following literature review explores the problem domain, solutions, and technologies to guide the creation of GameOn, a website and mobile application that will help people create teams and get matched with others based on skill levels, preferences, and location, which are the following key challenges.

2.2         Case Study

27 UTM students were surveyed, using google forms as the method of collecting responses. The google form was distributed across whatsapp groups. These groups play and organize sports weekly. They use whatsapp as their main organizing tool for their weekly game. Each of the respondents are an active and participating member of the weekly games.

The results indicated important obstacles to participating in sports. The survey showed that students have trouble finding teammates because of the skill level mismatch, the remote location of games, ineffective communication (e.g. chaotic WhatsApp groups), the absence of beginner-friendly opportunities, and the high cost or difficulty of booking venues (figure 1.0) . These results fit with our problem background in general, which highlighted the challenge of locating suitable sports partners. The survey results focus on the user preferences where 83% of the respondents considered the skill-based matching with teammates as very important, and 75% of the respondents valued the geolocation feature to find nearby players (figure 1.1) . This case study confirms the necessity of the main functions of GameOn, including AI-based skill and location-based matching and in-app team organization

               Figure 1.0 Challenges by teammates  

                 Figure 1.1 potential features

While we have also asked respondents about what the sports they want to initially see within the system, 73% of respondents have shown their first favor for football , Basketball came second at 46% , while volleyball got the third most wanted sport to be seen. Other activities such Hiking and Combat & Grappling Variants took the attention of only a few respondents.

                 Figure 1.2 potential features

        Through these responses we can confidently rank the most wanted sports among different respondents. We can conclude that football came first with 73% of the respondents choices, then almost half chose basketball, followed by volleyball , hiking and combat sports. Since we are focused on the local communities based in Malaysia it is fit to mention that according to the Badminton World Federation (BWF) Asia accounts for over 60% of the world's badminton players, with China, Indonesia, India, Japan, and Malaysia leading in participation (Global Badminton Participation Report. 2018)  through this context the system should also conclude badminton in the sports needed.

2.2.1 Company Organization Structure

The development team is led by Ali Mohamed, who handles technical implementation across all SDLC documentation phases. Requirements elicitation is achieved through surveys and feedback from end-users, including Anas Ayme Fahmey (a casual player) and Mohanad Altater (who has participated in many professional championships in football and other sports). Both stakeholders will also contribute to User Acceptance Testing (UAT). This ensures a user-centric development approach with clear responsibilities divided between technical development and end-user validation from both casual and competitive gaming perspectives.

2.2.2 Manual Operation

Currently, those wanting to play sports tend to connect with others through their own contacts, online groups, or messaging apps. They are inefficient and can cause problems, like having chaotic WhatsApp groups, not matching players based on their skills, and it being tough to find players or locations locally. The results of the survey found that half of the respondents have to deal with a lack of players who share their skill level, and 41% find the game lacks beginner-friendly options (figure 1.0). Interaction with players tends to be less personal without AI, and GameOn seeks to solve this by adding AI to the service. Because of these problems, there is a need for a centralized system that facilitates both forming teams and staying in contact.

2.3 Current System Analysis

Current platforms such as Meetup and playo are partial solutions and they do not fully meet the needs of sports enthusiasts. Meetup is an app that enables general meet-ups, but does not offer sport-specific functions and skill-based matching. Playo, a brand dedicated to racket sports in India, cannot support team sports, such as football, or outdoor activities, such as hiking, which makes it rather limited. Both platforms lack the incorporation of AI to offer personalized training suggestions and a holistic user experience based on different sports categories. The Game On proposal focuses on its distinction via AI tips performance, the possibility to cover a variety of sports types (team sports, racket sports, and outdoor activities), and an intuitive interface.

2.4  Comparison between existing systems

2.4.1 MeetUp ( existing system )

Although Meetup is a popular tool to organize general events, it has serious drawbacks when it comes to sports-related networking. The site does not have the matching skills, which is essential for sports enthusiasts who want to find partners who have the right skills. It has a weak geolocation search feature, whereby users have a hard time locating sports activities around them. The platform has no specialized categories of various sports, which makes the user experience of an athlete, who wants to connect with others in a specific sport, disjointed. Also, the communication within the app is limited to simple group chats, and there is no information about the venue, so the user cannot get the necessary information about playing places and facilities.

Figure 2.0 Meetup interface

2.4.2 Playo (existing System )

Plavo is more focused however, limited in scope and functionality. The platform offers partial skill-based matching only in racket sports that limits its users and its use in various sporting activities. Although Plavo has geolocation search and venue data like court availability, the app is too narrow in its racket sports focus and can only reach a limited number of users. Communication capabilities of the platform are also not extensive, and it does not have AI-based training tips or extensive customization options of user profiles that are expected by modern sports enthusiasts, as it is the case with Meetup.

Figure 2.1 Playo interface

2.4.3 Whatsapp ( existing System )

Most sports communities are now using WhatsApp groups to organize themselves, and this poses a big organizational problem. The WhatsApp groups are disorganized and several conversations are happening at the same time, and it is hard to follow particular game plans, levels of skill, or location information. The site does not have a well-organized sports-specific content, search on previous discussions, and appropriate classification of users according to their skills or preferences. The casual style can easily result in miscommunication, lost chances, and ineffective coordination between sports partners.

Figure 2.2  Whatsapp interface

table 1.0 compare existing systems

2.5  Literature Review of Technology Used

  • Frontend(Next.js) : Next.js is a React framework with server-side rendering and static site generation, which guarantees good load performance and optimization of SEO. Vercel (2024) states that Next.js can decrease page load tizmes by up to 50% relative to the conventional React apps, which would improve the user experience when using the web interface of GameOn.
  • Backend( Node.js -  Express.js ) : Node.js offers a highly scalable and event-driven framework that would be useful in real-time functionality such as notifying a team. Express.js is a framework that makes API creation easy, making it effective to communicate between the frontend and backend .
  • Database( MongoDB) : The NoSQL format of MongoDB would allow flexible data modelling of user profiles and team data. This is important in scaling up to large numbers of users as indicated by MongoDB Inc. (2024).
  • Maps Integration (Google Maps API) : This API will be used to implement geolocation-based search, which is essential in pairing the user with the teams nearby. According to Google Cloud (2024), the API offers accurate location data with 99.9 percent uptime.
  • AI ( DeepSeek API ) : offers natural language processing of personalized training tips. New NLP models (DeepSeek, 2024) will guarantee relevant and contextual suggestions, improving user interaction.
  • Security (JWT Encryption) : JSON Web Tokens provide secure user authentication, safeguarding sensitive data such as profiles and location data (Auth0, 2024).

2.6 Chapter Summary

This literature review demonstrates that the GameOn platform is needed, as it identifies the difficulties sports enthusiasts meet when seeking compatible teammates and the drawbacks of the available solutions.

The necessity of skill-based matching, geolocation, in-app communication is confirmed by the UTM student survey and stakeholder feedback. In comparison to Meetup and Plavo, GameOn is a more complete and customized solution due to the possibility of supporting many types of sports and AI-based functionalities.

The suggested technology stack (Next.js, Node.js, MongoDB, DeepSeek API) is appropriate to provide a convenient platform. The review will give GameOn a basis of development that will see it meet the needs of the users and also use the latest technologies to improve sports participation.

CHAPTER 3

SYSTEM DEVELOPMENT METHODOLOGY

3.1 Introduction

In the case of the GameOn sports team matching, a multi-featured web site needs a development approach that guarantees quality and requirement partnering. Monitoring and recording of progress at various stages would enable a systematic communication to all stakeholders. The methodology in this project will give a roadmap that stipulates the order of phases, activities and deliverables..

3.2 Methodology Choice and Justification

3.2.1 Methodology Selected: Waterfall Model

        The Waterfall methodology is going to be the main methodology for this project. The strict sequential and plan-driven approach that follows a linear progression, while cutting milestones and progressing only forward is going to be the most fit methodology for this project.

3.2.2 Justification Criteria

The choice of the Waterfall methodology to be used in the GameOn project is supported by a number of important criteria that are consistent with the nature of the project and the limitations. Since the project scope is clearly identified and the requirements are fixed with well-defined sports networking goals, the project is appropriate to sequential planning techniques. The user requirements are also stable with the basic needs of finding sports partners and teams being the same in the development process and the requirements are not expected to change significantly. Such stability enables the predictable flow that is typical of the Waterfall model, in which one phase is based on the work done in the previous phase. The personal character of the project, as well as a predetermined set of skills, makes the organized style of Waterfall especially beneficial in terms of managing the complexity.

The project academic setting is another reason why the Waterfall methodology is chosen. The need of UTM to have complete documentation such as Software Requirements Specification (SRS), Software Design Document (SDD), and technical documentation fits well with Waterfall that requires a lot of documentation at every stage of development. The stakeholder structure is characterized by a strict hierarchy with the project owner and a specific user base, which allows conducting scheduled reviews and approval procedures which are a part of the Waterfall approach. Moreover, the time limitations of having set semester deadlines and milestones submissions provide predictable stages that can be easily matched to the academic calendar, which guarantees that the project will be completed in a systematic manner and yet fulfil all academic and practical requirements.

Criteria

GameOn Project Context

Waterfall Alignment

Project Scope

Fixed requirements with clear sports networking objectives

 well defined scope suits sequential planning

Requirements Stability

Stable user needs (find sports partners and teams )

 Minimal requirement changes expected

Documentation Requirements

 UTM requires comprehensive SRS, SDD, and technical documentation

Emphasizes a thorough documentation at each phase

Stakeholder Involvement

supervisor, Owner and defined user base

 Clear stakeholder hierarchy with scheduled reviews

Timeline Constraints

Fixed semester deadlines and milestone submissions

Predictable phases align with academic calendar

Work Experience

Individual project with a preknown skill set

Structured approach reduces complexity management

                        Table 2.0 : system development Justification criteria

        

3.2.3  implementation of Methodology

In this section we are going to elaborate on the work flow of the chosen methodology. While each stage has its own set of checklists to be done the gantt chart at least covers the time and work span from and when each stage starts and ends.

each phase is thoroughly explained in the next section.

while also the gantt chart of phase 2 is provided in the appendix F.

3.3 Phases of the Chosen Methodology

3.3.1 Phase 1: Requirements Analysis and Specification

The Requirements Analysis and Specification is the initial phase of the software development lifecycle, during which the project team collects, analyzes, and documents all the requirements needed by the sports-oriented application. This detailed stage starts with the interviews of stakeholders and surveys of the users to learn the needs and expectations of the sports enthusiasts who will be the main users of the system. The development team undertakes a lot of functional and non-functional requirements gathering and both the specific features that the users require and the underlying system performance requirements are well identified and documented.

At this stage, the team develops user interface mockups and wireframes showing the visualization of how the application will appear and work through the user point of view. At the same time, the identification and documentation of use cases are performed, during which the team draws all the possible interactions between the system and users. These use cases are well validated and approved so that they are accurate and complete before proceeding.

The phase will be completed by drawing activity diagrams that will describe the flow of the most important processes in the application, such as user registration processes, event creation processes, team matching algorithms, and AI-based performance tip generation. The main products of this stage are the complete Software Requirements Specifications (SRS), detailed Use Case Diagrams, particular Use Case specifications, and Activity Diagrams that will serve as a blueprint of the whole development process.

The main deliverables of this stage include a set of detailed user stories that are specifically oriented toward sports fans, well-defined system limitations and performance demands, use case diagrams that are able to clearly demonstrate the interactions between actors within the system, improved UI/UX design specifications, and activity diagrams of key workflows such as user registration, event creation, team matching, and AI performance tip delivery systems. Such comprehensive requirements analysis provides a strong basis and direction of all the further development stages to develop a successful sports application.

3.3.2 Phase 2: System Design and Architecture

The System Design and Architecture plays the most important role between requirements gathering and the actual implementation process where the development team would convert the documented requirements into a full technical blueprint of the sports application. This step is aimed at developing a powerful and expandable system architecture capable of efficiently serving all the identified user requirements and functional needs and ensuring the highest performance levels.

The phase starts with the top-level system architecture design work, in which the architects and senior developers work together to determine the general structure and organization of the application components. This includes taking vital decisions regarding the modularity of the system, separation of components and the general technological strategy that will be used to drive the whole development process. At the same time, the team is involved in the process of database design and entity relationship modeling, which is a data structure that will effectively store and manage all information related to sports, user accounts, events, and performance statistics.

The planning of API design and integration will make it possible to ensure that the system is able to communicate with the external services and offer effective data exchange capabilities. The team is especially careful about security and data privacy design, where strong measures are taken to safeguard sensitive data about users and to adhere to the applicable data protection laws. Interaction diagrams of the system components are drawn to illustrate the manner in which various components of the application will interact and collaborate to provide the desired functionality.

The main output of this stage is a detailed System Design Document (SDD) which is the technical roadmap of the development, a detailed Technical Architecture specification which defines the structural components of the system, and a full database Schema which defines the data storage and relationship structures. The most important deliverables include elaborate system architecture diagrams that will give visual illustration of how the application will be technically structured, extensive database schema documentation that will give clear relationship between entities and finalization of the technology stack that will be utilized during the development process. This comprehensive design stage provides the developer with a clear technical direction and strong architectural basis to develop a stable, secure, and efficient sports application.

3.3.3 Phase 3: Implementation and Development

         The Implementation and Development symbolizes the main implementation phase of the software development lifecycle, in which the theoretical designs and specifications of the earlier phases are converted to a working sports application. This is the most intense stage that entails the actual coding and building of all system components, where frontend interfaces, backend services, and database systems are integrated into a single and functional platform that supports the interests of sports enthusiasts.

The development process starts with the frontend development based on wireframes, where the user interface team develops responsive and intuitive screens that enable a user to interact with the application features without any difficulties. This involves the introduction of user registration screens, event browsing, team matching screens, and AI-based performance tips screens. At the same time, backend development operations with Node.js and Express.js are aimed at developing the server-side code that will handle user requests, business rules, and data flow across the system.

Database implementation and setup tasks entail the configuration of MongoDB as the main data storage option, leveraging its document-based flexibility to effectively manage various sports-related data, such as user profiles, event data, team data, and performance data. The team carries out a lot of API development and integration, building strong communication channels between various system elements and external services. Special emphasis is placed on the implementation of security features, and developers implement the JWT (JSON Web Token) authentication system to implement secure user sessions, as well as data encryption and privacy protection strategies throughout the application.

The process of code documentation and version control makes sure that all the development work is properly tracked, documented, and available to be updated in the future, with GitHub being the central source code repository, collaborative development, and the source of the complete history of the project. The main deliverables of this stage are working application modules that show the main functionality, full source code that is the basis of the system, and technical documentation that describes the implementation choices and how the system works.

3.3.4 Phase 4: Testing and Quality Assurance

The Testing and Quality Assurance is the most important verification step of the software development lifecycle, during which the sports application developed is thoroughly tested to confirm that it has been developed to satisfy all the requirements and quality standards. This systematic stage includes various testing techniques and quality control procedures that aim at detecting, recording, and fixing any flaws or performance problems prior to the application being delivered to end users. The testing stage is the last gateway to ensure that all non-functional and functional requirements have been implemented correctly and the system works effectively under different conditions.

Unit testing of individual components is the first stage of the testing process whereby each discrete module of the application is tested to make sure that it is working properly in isolation. This fine-grained method enables the detection and correction of the problems at component level before they can spread to other system components. After unit testing, integration testing of system modules is done, which is aimed at ensuring that various components integrate well with each other upon combination. This step is especially important to the sports app, because it will test the connection between the Next.js frontend, Node.js backend, MongoDB database, and JWT authentication system.

User Acceptance Testing (UAT) is a fundamental part of the test plan, and consists of real sports enthusiasts who will be the target user group. The project has signed collaboration agreements with Mohanad and Anas Aymen who have officially accepted to be involved in the UAT process as per the email communication. This practical testing methodology involving dedicated testers gives a good idea of how the application will be usable, functional, and the overall user experience as perceived by the people who will eventually use the system. The joint UAT process will make sure that the feedback is provided by real users who are familiar with the sports field and can offer valuable comments regarding the system performance and user experience.

figure 3.0 UAT end-user agreement 1
figure 3.1 UAT end-user agreement 2

Security testing and vulnerability assessment are aimed at detecting possible security vulnerabilities and making sure that JWT authentication system and other security mechanisms are sufficient to protect user data. This is a thorough security analysis that involves the testing of general vulnerabilities, data encryption procedures, and privacy protection mechanisms to ensure that they are operating as expected. During the testing period, the process of bug fixing and system optimization is in constant progress and the problems are solved and the performance is enhanced according to the test outcomes.

The major deliverables of this phase are elaborate test plans which describe testing strategies and approaches, elaborate test results which records all testing activities and their results, elaborate bug reports which records the identified bugs and their status of being resolved, and quality measures which give quantitative measures of the system performance and reliability. The main deliverables include a test coverage report that shows the coverage of tests conducted on all the system components, a detailed user feedback and system enhancements based on the UAT results of the joint testing with Mohanad and Anas Aymen.

3.3.5 Phase 5: Deployment and Maintenance

The Deployment and Maintenance presents the last phase of the software development lifecycle, in which the well-tested GameOn sports application is passed into production, and is made available to the end users. This is the most critical stage that is concerned with stabilizing the production environment and making sure that the application works well in the real world and offers extensive support materials to the users and continuous system maintenance.

Deployment process starts with a production environment setup, in which the required infrastructure is prepared to host the GameOn application. This includes the creation of secure server environments, configuration of the database connections to MongoDB, and the implementation of JWT authentication systems, as well as the integration of all the elements of the technology stack, such as the Next.js frontend and the Node.js backend. The deployment and configuration of the application include copying the code of the tested application to the production servers and setting all the system parameters to the best performance, such as database migration, setting up API endpoints, and installing security certificates.

Training of users and preparation of documentation are the vital parts of the deployment stage, as it guarantees that sports fans will be able to use all the possibilities of the GameOn application. This will include the creation of detailed user guides that will help users to register, create events, match teams and receive AI-driven performance advice. The activities of system monitoring and performance tracking provide permanent monitoring mechanisms to make sure that the application remains to work successfully even after deployment, whereas the post-deployment support planning activity includes the development of the procedures that should be used to answer the questions of the users and introduce the updates to the system according to the feedback of the users.

The main outputs of this stage are the released application that gives users the access to the full GameOn system, user manuals that will help users to use all functions of the application, and maintenance documentation that will assist in the further work of the system. The major deliverables include the live GameOn application that will be used by the sports community with all the functionalities, user guides and documentation that would facilitate the effective use of the system, and the deployment and maintenance procedures that would guarantee the long-term reliability of the system and the satisfaction of the users. This deployment and implementation step will guarantee that the sports application will be effectively moved to the production environment and will deliver actual value to the users, and will form the basis of the future system maintenance and improvement.

Figure


3.4 Technology Used Description

3.4.1 Frontend Technologies

3.4.1.1 Next.js Framework.

Next.js was chosen as the main frontend framework because it is based on React and has the ability to offer server-side rendering (SSR) and static site generation (SSG). This framework provides superior performance attributes with high-speed rendering, automatic code splitting, and in-built optimization features that are critical to the user experience and involvement needs of the GameOn sports application.

3.4.1.2 React

React is the core library that offers a component-based framework that enhances code reusability and maintainability. The large ecosystem around React provides many third-party libraries (etc. MUI ) and community support that speed up the development process and allow the development of modular, maintainable code that can be used in the changing needs of the GameOn application.

3.4.1.3 TypeScript Implementation

Integration of TypeScript offers the possibility of static typing to JavaScript, thus increasing the reliability of the code and acting as a complete documentation. The static typing system detects the possible errors at the development stage instead of the runtime, and this fact reduces the number of bugs and enhances the stability of the application performance refactoring features of the GameOn platform.

Technology

Justification

Next.Js

React-based framework with SSR/SSG capabilities, excellent performance. Fast Rendering.

React

Component-based architecture, extensive ecosystem, maintainable code

TypeScript

Static typing for JavaScript, enhanced code reliability and documentation, less bugs

Table 3.1 Frontend justifications

3.4.2 Backend Technologies

3.4.2.1 Node.js Environment

The backend runtime environment was chosen as Node.js to ensure consistency in the JavaScript ecosystem across the whole stack of the application. This decision obviates the necessity of switching between the contexts of various programming languages, simplifying the development process and making it less complex. Node.js has a rich library support with npm ecosystem, which has a variety of packages and modules that help to speed up the development process and guarantee stable performance of the server-side functions of the GameOn sports application.

3.4.2.2 Express.js Framework

Express.js is the web application framework because of its minimal and flexible nature that enables a quick development without any unnecessary restrictions. The large middleware ecosystem offered by the framework offers ready-made solutions to many of the typical web application needs, like authentication, logging, and request handling. The lightweight and powerful middleware support of Express.js make it a perfect choice to develop API endpoints and server-side logic of the GameOn application.

3.4.2.3 MongoDB Database System

MongoDB was selected as the key database solution due to its NoSQL database architecture which offers flexible data storage features that are necessary in the management of various user profiles and event data. The schema can be easily changed using the document-based structure, and the document-based structure can support different data needs of sports events, user preferences, and application features. The flexibility of MongoDB allows it to get rid of the strict structures of the relational databases, and to store and retrieve the complex data relationships of the GameOn application efficiently.

Technology

Justification

Node.js

JavaScript ecosystem consistency, extensive library support

Express

Minimal and flexible web framework, extensive middleware ecosystem

MongoDB

NoSQL database for flexible user and event data storage

Table 3.1 Backend Justification

3.4.3 Additional Tools and APIs

3.4.3.1 Geolocation Services

The application is primarily using Google Maps API as its geolocation service, which allows extensive location-based capabilities and the ability to discover partners. This combination enables the user to get proper mapping services, location identification, and processing of geographic data. The API offers core spatial features that help to drive the fundamental functionality of the platform, which is connecting the users with the relevant partners and events around them.

3.4.3.2 AI Integration

The DeepSeek API provides artificial intelligence capabilities that provide advanced performance insights and personal recommendations to users. This AI integration examines the behavioral patterns, preferences, and history of users to come up with valuable insights that improve user experience. The system uses machine learning algorithms to make intelligent suggestions and performance analytics that are responsive to personal user needs and preferences.

3.4.3.3 Authentication

JSON Web Tokens (JWT) are used to provide a secure and strong authentication system, where user authentication and security are handled. JWT offers stateless authentication which guarantees secure user management and dependable session management within the application. This authentication system is token-based, and it is secure and allows smooth user experiences across various application components and services.

3.4.3.4 Database Infrastructure

Its database infrastructure runs on MongoDB Atlas, a cloud-based NoSQL database platform that offers the scalability and flexibility to support increasing user bases and growing data demands. MongoDB Atlas provides automatic scaling, backup, and global distribution that guarantees the best performance and data availability. The NoSQL design meets the flexibility of data models and fast development cycle required by the application

Component

Technology

Purpose

Geolocation

Google Map API

allowing for close searches and filters.

AI Integration

DeepSeek API

Performance insights and recommendations

Authentication

JWT

Secure user management and session handling

Database

MongoDB Atlas

Cloud-hosted NoSQL database for scalability

Table 3.2 Additional Tools and APIs

3.5 System Requirement Analysis

3.5.1 Requirements Gathering Strategy

The GameOn platform requirements gathering process is a multi-faceted requirements gathering process that is aimed at providing an in-depth knowledge of user requirements and expectations of the system. The most important data collection tool is user surveys, which will be conducted among the UTM students who are sporting enthusiasts with varying fitness levels and competitive abilities. In this way, it will be possible to represent different levels of skills and sports preferences, which will help to understand the diverse needs of the target user base. Interviews with identified end-users are also carried out to obtain detailed feedback and confirm assumptions regarding user behavior and expectations. 

The systematic elaboration of use cases illustrates the interaction of the users with the system, particularly on two main types of users, Admin and Player, with different functionality and interaction with the system, which fully covers the functionality of the platform.

  • User Surveys: Attract sporting fans of UTM students with various fitness and competitive skills
  • Stakeholder Interviews: Specified end-users.
  • Use Case Development: Systematic Approach to show how the user interacts with the system by having two types of users (Admin , Player) with the various features of the system.

3.5.2 Functional Requirements Categories

The functional requirements are grouped into five main categories covering the fundamental system capabilities. User Management involves registration procedures and profile creation including preferences of sports and level of skills. Team Management offers full control over the team activities, such as create, edit, manage, and delete. Team Discovery allows searching a team based on location and has advanced filtering to find an appropriate partner. AI Integration uses the DeepSeek API to provide individual performance suggestions and training recommendations. Admin Functions provide the platform integrity by moderating the user and managing the content.

  • User Management :Registration, profile creation with sports preferences and skill levels
  • Team Management :  Create, edit, manage, and delete teams
  • Team Discovery:  Location-based team search along with filtering capabilities
  • AI Integration:   Performance tips and training recommendations via (DeepSeek API)
  • Admin Functions: User moderation and content management 

3.5.3 Non-Functional Requirements Focus

Non-functional requirements draw important performance and quality measures towards the best user experience. Performance requirements focus on efficiency of team matching, aiming at casual players to locate teams in three clicks. The usability standards focus on the clarity and intuitive interface design of the user with low web literacy. The security implementation is aimed at JWT-based authentication and data protection in general. GPS integration through Location Services allows accurate geographic team filtering, allowing users to find local sporting opportunities efficiently.

  • Performance: Quick team matching (<3 clicks for casual players)
  • Usability: Clear interface suitable for users with basic web literacy
  • Security: JWT-based authentication and data protection
  • Location Services: GPS integration for geographic team filtering

3.6 Chapter Summary

Matching development of the GameOn Sport team has chosen the Waterfall methodology due to its relevance to the project requirements, which are stable, academic documentation requirements, and strict deadlines constraints. This phased method will offer a methodological framework that is required in a systematic development and at the same time provide an extensive documentation and deliverables that are up to standards.

CHAPTER 4

REQUIREMENT ANALYSIS AND DESIGN

4.1 Introduction

In this chapter we shall give a complete discussion of the requirements of the GameOn Sports Matching site and the design of the system. We are going to cover the database design as well as the user interface design.

4.2 Requirement Analysis

The requirements are categorized into two major parts that include functional and non-functional requirements. In Addition to, that we have priority flag for the basic system requirement

4.2.1 Functional Requirements Analysis

Functional requirements within the system are spread into five modules.  

                

4.2.1.1 User Management Module

The User Management Module is the core of user interaction in the GameOn platform and offers the full account management and security functionality. User Registration allows new users to sign in to the platform by creating an account using email and password verification to validate their first access to the platform. Profile Management enables the user to have a detailed profile that consists of such information as sports preference, skill level, and location data, which leads to personalized experience based on the needs and interests of the user. The Authentication and Authorization feature is used to secure the login and log out processes with strong session management features to secure user data and ensure system integrity.

Strict requirements validation is also integrated within the module to provide quality and security of data. Registration is done to ensure that there are no duplicate accounts and to ensure that the passwords used are strong by ensuring that the password criteria are met. To avoid unauthorized profile update and integrity of data, profile updates are only initiated by the user who needs to authenticate himself. The system is compatible with role-based access control, where the access of the system is divided into two roles, namely, Player and Admin, each of which has corresponding permissions and access levels based on the functional areas of responsibility in the system.

  • User Registration: New users create accounts with email/password authentication
  • Profile Management: Users maintain profiles including sports preferences, skill levels, and location data
  • Authentication & Authorization: Secure login/logout functionality with session management

Requirements Validation:

  • Registration process must validate email uniqueness and password strength
  • Profile updates require user authentication
  • System must support role-based access (Player, Admin)

4.2.1.2 Team Management Module

The Team Management Module offers a wide range of team management functions that facilitate users to build, control and sustain their sporting teams. Team Creation enables users to create teams with their own parameters depending on their sporting needs and preferences. Team Information Editing feature allows users to edit the information and settings of teams as required to keep teams up to date and accurate. Member Management functionalities enable the owners of a team to add and remove team members to ensure that teams have the right composition and dynamics. The Team Deletion feature offers the opportunity to shut down owned teams when they are needed and the Join Request Processing allows the team owners to accept or reject the applications of players, making sure that they are the right ones to be a part of the team.

The module has requirements validation in order to ensure organizational structure and integrity of the system. Every user will be able to have only one primary team ownership, and this will not allow conflicting resources and will enable concentrated team management. The key details that should be incorporated in team parameters are the type of sport, the level of skills required, location, and type of game which will enable the teams to have detailed profiles that will make matching and discovery easy to be achieved. Only team owners have member management functions and this ensures that there are no unauthorized changes of teams which may cause a disruption to the team operations.

  •  Team Creation: Users create teams with customizable parameters
  • Team Information Editing: Modify team details and settings
  •  Member Management: Add/remove team members
  • Team Deletion: Disband owned teams
  • Join Request Processing: Accept/reject player applications

Requirements Validation:

  • Each user limited to one primary team ownership
  • Team parameters must include sport type, skill level requirements, location, and game type
  • Member management restricted to team owners only

4.2.1.3 Matching and Search Module

The Matching and Search Module offers advanced search features to find out matching teams and players in an efficient manner. Geographic-based search uses the integration of GPS so as to provide location-based results to users based within their preferred geographic proximity. High-level filtering allows narrowing down the search by several filters such as sport type, skill level, age, gender, and location. Instant feedback and precise distance estimation based on real-time search results using location proximity allow making informed decisions.

The module has full requirement validation to serve the best functionality. Location-based features necessitate the use of GPS permission to enable correct geographic matching. Search filters are persistent across user sessions, and preferences are preserved, and redundant configuration is avoided. The matching algorithm takes into account various compatibility criteria in simultaneous fashion, generating comprehensive evaluations in order to improve the quality of matches rather than just based on proximity.

  • Geographic-based team/player search using GPS integration
  • Advanced filtering by sport type, skill level, age, gender, location
  • Real-time search results with location proximity

Requirements Validation:

  • GPS permission required for location-based features
  • Search filters must be persistent during user sessions
  • Matching algorithm considers multiple compatibility factor

4.2.1.4 AI Training Module

The AI Integration Module makes use of artificial intelligence that enables personalized performance guidance to increase user experience. The DeepSeek API enables the integration of the third-party NLP AI models to offer sophisticated natural language processing features to intelligent user interactions. The training tips are personalized depending on what the user inputs and provide personalized training tips depending on the user needs and level of skills. The generation of action plans based on challenges produces strategic actions that can help users to deal with particular sporting problems, whereas the performance improvement suggestions give practical advice on how to work on skills and athletic performance.

The validation in this module is to guarantee quality and security standards. The AI response should be sport-specific and contextual, being accurate and useful in the sporting activities of the users. API communication is important in integration to ensure the security of user data and integrity of the system in case of interactions with external services. The tips given during training are saved to ensure that the user can go back to the recommendations and monitor their progress over the years.

  • Integration with third-party NLP AI models (DeepSeek API)
  • Personalized training tips based on user input
  • Challenge-specific action plan generation
  • Performance enhancement recommendations

Requirements Validation:

  • AI responses must be sport-specific and contextually relevant
  • Integration requires secure API communication
  • Training tips stored for user reference

4.2.1.5 Administration Module

The Administration Module offers full administrative tools to ensure platform integrity and safety of users. Moderation and deletion of user accounts feature allows administrators to control the user accounts, such as suspending or permanently deleting accounts that may be breaking platform policies. The team management and handling of violation capabilities enable administrators to monitor team activities, handle violations of policies, and sustain proper sporting environments. Report processing and resolution capabilities offer systematic processing of user reports in order to investigate and take action on a report within a reasonable amount of time to ensure community safety.

There should be authentication and logging of admin operations to ensure security and have audit trails of all administrative operations. The handling of reports should have a user notification system to update the affected parties on the progress of investigations and results of the resolutions. Deletion operations must involve confirmation procedures to avoid unintentional deletions and guarantee informed administrative actions to safeguard user information and platform stability.

Figure 3.2 Use Case Diagram for <GameOn sports team matching>

4.2.2 Non-Functional Requirements Analysis

4.2.2.1  Usability Requirements

The Usability Requirements lay down the guidelines of the user experience and interface design to accommodate the highest level of platform accessibility and usability. The User Interface is focused on being intuitive and having only up to 3 clicks to match with a team, simplifying the main feature and eliminating user friction. The features of accessibility are mobile responsive design that supports both the mobile and desktop platform so that the user experience remains the same across the devices and screen sizes. 

  • User Interface: Intuitive design requiring maximum 3 clicks for team matching
  • Accessibility: Responsive design supporting mobile and desktop platforms
  • Learning Curve: New users should complete registration and team joining within 10 minutes
  • Error Handling: Clear error messages and recovery options

4.2.2.2  Security Requirements

Security Requirements outline the complete user data and system integrity protection. Authentication is based on secure session management with JWT, which offers stateless authentication with high levels of security and with the ability to offer a continuous user experience across platform components. Data Protection also involves encryption of sensitive data of users whose personal information is kept safe and in the process of data transmission and storage, the privacy of the users is maintained. Privacy protection schemes: The user location data can be anonymized, preserving the user geographic information and still being able to use location-based features. Access Control sets privileges of the various types of users and ensures that they have the right level of access and cannot do anything that may negatively affect the security of the system or the security of a user.

  • Authentication: JWT-based secure session management
  • Data Protection: Encryption of sensitive user information
  • Privacy: User location data anonymization
  • Access Control: Role-based permissions for different user types

4.2.3 Requirements Prioritization

High Priority (Must Have)

  • User registration and authentication
  • Team creation and management
  • Basic search and filtering
  • Location-based matching

Medium Priority (Should Have)

  • AI training recommendations
  • Advanced filtering options
  • Admin moderation tools
  • Performance optimization

Low Priority (Could Have)

  • Social features and messaging
  • Advanced analytics
  • Mobile application development
  • Integration with external calendar systems

 

4.3 Project Design

4.3.1 System Architecture

GameOn Sports Matching is an application based on the Model-View-Controller (MVC) architectural pattern, which is a web-based application. This architecture guarantees separation of concerns, maintainability and scalability and fits within the technical constraints of the project.

Architecture Components:

GameOn Sports architecture uses Model-View-Controller (MVC) architecture with a well defined separation of concerns and flexible code structuring. The system is made of a three component architecture with these parts interacting to provide full sports networking capability.

View layer is a layer of presentation, where it is rendered with responsibilities to provide user interface and user interaction. It contains the authentication ( AuthView ), user profiles ( ProfileView ), game management ( GameView, GameCreationView, GameEditView ), the team activities ( MembersView, RequestView ), system administration ( AdminView ), and the user communications ( NotificationView, ReportsView ). Each aspect of the view is devoted to particular user interface duties and patterns of user actions.

Controller layer is between the View and model layer and receives user input and marshals application logic. AuthController controls the authentication of users, ProfileController deals with a profile of users, GameController deals with team and game related functionality, ReportController processes report of a user and NotificationsController deals with delivery of notifications to the users. Views feed user inputs to the controllers, process the business logic and liaise with the right models so as to meet the requests.

The Model layer gathers the data and business logic, such as UserModel to handle accounts, GameModel to provide team and game data, ValidationService to apply business rules, NotificationModel to manage alert systems and ReportModel to manage administrative flows. The communication between the components is structured and organized according to defined MVC patterns, wherein user requests are sent via Views to Controllers, which interact with the Model to process user requests and send the corresponding responses back to a View to reflect data transformations.

4.3.2 MVC Architecture Implementation

figure 4.0 MVC Architecture

4.3.2.1 Model Layer (Data Management)

The layer of the model turns out to be the core of data processing and realization of business logic. It includes six fundamental models that deal with various application activities: User Model can be used in authentication, profile management; Game Model can be used in game lifecycle, Team Model can be used to manage team composition, Player Model can be used to trace participation, Report Model can be used to moderate contents, and Notification Model can be used to raise system alerts.

This layer implements key tasks such as validation and cleansing of the data, schema definition of the database with its correctly defined relationships, business rules such as single owner of a team per user and optimized in a data persistence operation. The model layer is specially apt in location-based query optimization, and it guarantees effectiveness of search in terms of proximity games and teams as well as data integrity in all the operations.

Core Models:

  1. User Model: Manages user authentication, profile data, sports preferences, and skill levels
  2. Game Model: Handles game creation, modification, and status management
  3. Team Model: Manages team composition within games (junction entity)
  4. Player Model: Tracks user participation in teams and games
  5. Report Model: Handles user-submitted reports for content moderation
  6. Notification Model: Manages system notifications and alerts

Model Responsibilities:

  • Data validation and sanitization
  • Database schema definition and relationships
  • Business logic enforcement (e.g., one team ownership per user)
  • Data persistence and retrieval operations
  • Query optimization for location-based searches

4.3.2.2 View Layer (Presentation)

The view layer provides responsive user-friendly interface based on the principle of the 3-click maximum navigation. It has seven main segments Authentication Views to manage user access, Profile Management to personalize a user, Game Management dashboard to work on a team level, Search Interface with complex filtering option, AI Training Hub to work though personal recommendation, Admin Dashboard to maintain moderation activities, and Notification Center to get updated information in real-time.

Which concentrates on responsive layouts in both mobile and desktop environments and deals with form validation and processing of user entries, is the view layer. It keeps primitive, real-time updates through state-of-the-art state management, gives good error messages and feedback to the user, and remains completely accessible in order to support all the users.

Core Views/Components:

  1. Authentication Views: Registration, login, password recovery
  2. Profile Management: User profile creation and editing
  3. Game Management: Game creation, editing, member management dashboard
  4. Search Interface: Advanced filtering and location-based team discovery
  5. AI Training Hub: Personalized training recommendations display
  6. Admin Dashboard: User and game moderation interface
  7. Notification Center: Real-time updates and alerts

View Responsibilities:

  • Responsive design for mobile and desktop platforms
  • Form validation and user input handling
  • Real-time updates using state management
  • Error message display and user feedback
  • Accessibility compliance

4.3.2.3 Controller Layer (Application Logic)

The controller layer facilitates the interplay between models and views and is in charge of HTTP requests, as well as business processes. There are seven dedicated controllers, including AuthController to manage user authentication and sessions, UserController to perform profile functionality, and GameController to run game CRUD operations, SearchController to filter and match, and game profiles, AIController to connect to an AI API DeepSeek, AdminController to moderate, and NotificationController to respond to alerts in real-time.

The layer deals with critical tasks like route request and parameter validation, business logic sequencing among various components, the full API endpoint gestures and external service connection. Security protocols and authorization checks are also allowed at the controller layer as all the interactions of the user on an application should be secure and duly authenticated during the application running cycles in general.

Core Controllers:

  1. AuthController: Handles user authentication, registration, and session management
  2. UserController: Manages profile operations and user preferences
  3. GameController: Processes game CRUD operations and team management
  4. SearchController: Handles filtering, location-based queries, and matching algorithms
  5. AIController: Integrates with DeepSeek API for training recommendations
  6. AdminController: Manages user moderation and report processing
  7. NotificationController: Handles real-time notifications and alerts

Controller Responsibilities:

  • Request routing and parameter validation
  • Business logic coordination
  • API endpoint management
  • External service integration
  • Security enforcement and authorization

4.4 Database Design

4.4.1 ERD Diagram

The GameOn Sports Team Matching system Entity Relationship Diagram is a data model that reflects the entire complex relationships between users, games, teams, and related activities. The ERD is hierarchical in nature with users being the central entity and related to other entities by well defined relationships that ensure data integrity and business logic of the system.

Entities and their Functions

- User Entity

is the core of the system, which contains the necessary data about people communicating with the platform. This object stores personal data, authentication information, skill levels, and user categories, which means that it is the main source of reference of all activities regarding the user. The move to concentrate user data in one place was taken to guarantee consistency in data and make it easy to manage users within the platform.

- Game Entity

 is the main business object of the system, which has all the information that is required to describe a sporting event or match. This object contains logistical information including location and time, technical information including team limits and skill requirements, and operational information including current status and availability. The game entity is a container, which coordinates all the activities and players.

-Team Entity

is the organization structure of games. Instead of assigning users to games directly, the system employs teams as an intermediate structure, enabling flexible teams and a well-balanced gameplay. This architectural choice facilitates the need of the system to have organized team based sports as well as ensuring that the system is scaled to support other forms of games.

-Player Entity

The many-to-many relationship between users and teams is solved by the use of a junction table(Player Entity) . This object keeps the details of participation and ensures referential integrity between user accounts and team memberships. The individual player object enables the history of participation to be tracked in detail and enables more advanced features such as player statistics and analysis of performance.

-Sports Entity

        This entity will be used by two main entities, contributing to the user profile, allowing the user to create different skill levels for each sport within the system. While on the game entity will use the sports entity allowing the game to have different team maximum number for each game accordingly, and might as well have different rules depending on the gametype.

                 Figure 4.1 ERD diagram <GameOn sports team matching>

   

4.4.2 Database Schema

 User Table :

Figure 4.2 User Table Schema

Games Table :

Figure 4.3 Games Table Schema

Teams Table :

Figure 4.3 Teams Table Schema

Players Table :

Figure 4.4  Plays Table Schema

Join_request Table :

Figure 4.5 Join Request Table Schema

Reports Table :

Figure 4.6 Reports Table Schema

4.4.3  Database Security

Data Encryption strategies must consider data storage and processing. Passwords and other sensitive data are supposed to be encrypted with strong hashing algorithms, whereas personal data may need to be encrypted at the field level. The database connections must implement the SSL/TLS encryption to secure data transmission.

4.5 Interface Design

User View :  

Login and Sign up

 Home Page and Dashboard  

 

View Teams & Reports

Upcoming Games and Game view  

 

AI Coach

4.6 Chapter Summary

This chapter presents the major requirements and architecture design of the GameOn Sports Matching system. It outlines both functional requirements in five core modules (User, Team, Matching & Search, AI Training, and Administration) and non-functional requirements such as usability (3-click navigation) and security (JWT authentication). 

The design of the project is based on the MVC architecture, and the tech stack includes React.js, Node.js/Express.js, and MongoDB. It also combines external APIs (DeepSeek AI, GPS) and has an ERD diagram of database structure, with an emphasis on security (encryption, SSL/TLS). Finally, interface mockups present the experience of using the system in terms of login, dashboard and AI coach.

CHAPTER 5

CONCLUSION

5.1 Introduction

GameOn Sports team matching project is a complete solution to the long-standing problems of sports enthusiasts who have to struggle to find teammates that are suitable to them and to create a team with them. With the help of a comprehensive research, methodological approach to development and well-thought design, this project has managed to develop a framework of a web-based platform that will fill the gap between individual players and participation in organized team sports.

5.2 Achievement of Project Objectives

GameOn Sports Team Matcher project has been able to meet its main requirements by providing a complete solution to the main problem noted in the original problem statement.

5.2.1 Stakeholder Analysis and the Requirements specification

The project was able to elicit detailed requirements using various methods such as interviews of stakeholders, user surveys, and a methodical study of current solutions. The overall requirements analysis led to the identification of five core functional modules: User Management, Team Management, Matching and Search, AI Training, and Administration. The stakeholder analysis also outlined the roles and responsibilities, including the primary investor Mohamed Elbermawy, the development team, and the end-users, and made the process user-centric.

5.2.2 Development of User Profiles and Preferences to Sports

The system design has been able to integrate extensive user profiling features which enable the players to define their sports interests, their level of skills (beginner, intermediate, advanced), and their preferences. The user management module offers strong authentication and authorization functionalities whereby users can have detailed profiles that act as the basis of the matching algorithm. The profile system also allows role-based access control, with the difference between regular players and administrators, and ensures data privacy and security, using JWT-based authentication.

5.2.3 Team Creation and Location-Based Services

Team management functionality has been well developed to facilitate complete team creation and management features. The parameters that can be customized by the users to form teams are the type of sport, the level of skills required, location preferences and scheduling games. The use of Google Maps API provides accurate geolocation services. The system allows the use of sophisticated filtering criteria by location, skill level, age, gender and sport type and the user can find the best matches.

5.2.4 Comprehensive System Architecture

The project has implemented a strong MVC (Model-View-Controller) structure that guarantees separation of concerns, maintainability and scalability. The database design, expressed in an elaborate ERD diagram, creates a clear relationship between users, games, teams, and players and preserves data integrity.

5.2.5 Technology Stack Implementation

The selected technology stack is the one that fits perfectly to the project. Next.js has server-side rendering and static site generation features to support the best performance, and Node.js and Express.js will allow scaling backend services. The NoSQL structure of MongoDB supports the scalable data needs of team and user profiles. Modern security measures such as JWT encryption and SSL/TLS communication are incorporated to guarantee the security of data and privacy of the user.

5.2.6 User Experience Design

The interface design is able to meet the usability need of allowing no more than 3-clicks to perform the core functions. The reactive design is accessible on mobile and desktop devices, which makes it accessible to various devices. The user interface mockups show an easy way to navigate the process of registration to the assignment to a team, as well as easy controls of managing a team, setting a game schedule, and with the help of AI recommendations on how to train.

5.3 Suggestions for Future Improvement

Although the GameOn Sports Team Matcher project has managed to create a strong basis in the field of matching sports teams, multiple improvement and expansion opportunities have been noticed to further develop the possibilities of the platform and experience of its users.

Development in the AI integration and using machine learning algorithms to understand users behavior patterns, team performance measures, and seasonal trends to make more accurate team recommendations and the best suggestions on game scheduling

.

Although the existing web-based application responds to the mobile device, the creation of mobile applications would benefit the user engagement greatly. In addition, Increasing the social component of the platform would help create a more active community. Meaning to include an In App Messaging feature; which should have the comprehensive communication with team chat rooms, individual messaging and group conversations to enhance coordination and team building.

Key outputs encompass a functional mobile application that users can download and use for their sports activities, robust backend services and APIs that handle all server-side processing requirements, an integrated MongoDB database system that efficiently stores and manages all application data using flexible document structures, and comprehensive JWT-based authentication and security features that provide secure user sessions and protect user information throughout the system. This development phase transforms conceptual designs into tangible software solutions that provide real value to sports enthusiasts seeking to enhance their athletic experiences through technology.

Software Requirements Specification (SRS)

GameOn Sports Matching Teams

Version 1.0

Prepared by: Ali Mohamed Fathy Abdelkader Elbermawy

Revision Page

Overview

This SRS document gives a detailed specification of the GameOn Sports Teams Matching Application, a web-based application that will help sports enthusiasts to find the right teams according to their skill level, location, and preferences. The document outlines such functional requirements as user registration, team creation and management, location-based matching, and AI-based training recommendations.

  1. Target Audience

This Document is supervised by Dr. Rohayanti bt. Hassan, Whom is the main supervisor, While the requirement gatherings will be extracted from the stakeholders (Owner , End-user)

Examples of models are from Arlow and Neustadt (2002) and other sources stated accordingly.
